steps: In right triangle XYZ with the right angle at Z, the two acute angles X and Y satisfy \(X + Y = 90^\circ\), making them complementary.
By the co-function identity, \(\sin X = \cos(90^\circ - X) = \cos Y\). So \(\sin X\) equals \(\cos Y\).

Verify directly using sides: label the sides as \(XZ = a\), \(YZ = b\), hypotenuse \(XY = c\). Then \(\sin X = \frac{b}{c}\) (opposite over hypotenuse). For angle Y, adjacent = \(YZ = b\) and hypotenuse = \(c\), so \(\cos Y = \frac{b}{c}\). Confirmed equal.
